headline: Preparing for DDoS Attacks or Just Groundhog Day
snippet: A number of companies help Web sites fight basic cyber attacks and unexpected traffic, but most are prohibitively expensive. CloudFlare, a two-year-old San Francisco start-up, helps defend Web sites from cyber attacks on the cheap. Two years in, it counts Groundhog.org, Metallica and Stratfor as customers.

headline: Baidu and CloudFlare Boost Users Over China’s Great Firewall
snippet: The Chinese search giant Baidu and CloudFlare, a start-up based in San Francisco, have joined in an unusual business arrangement to speed Internet traffic into and out of China.

headline: Matthew Prince of Cloudflare on the Dangers of Fast Growth
snippet: The chief executive of a cybersecurity firm says that adding more employees too quickly can erode the foundations of company culture.

headline: A Failure Here, Damaged Fiber There and a Day of Internet Glitches
snippet: Cloudflare and Google dealt with issues that affected countless sites and users on Tuesday.

headline: ‘Shut the Site Down,’ Says the Creator of 8chan, a Megaphone for Gunmen
snippet: The site is a venue for extremists to test out ideas. Cloudflare, one of 8chan’s service providers, has already taken action.

headline: Why Banning 8chan Was So Hard for Cloudflare: ‘No One Should Have That Power’
snippet: 8chan, the anonymous message board used by the El Paso suspect, went offline, at least temporarily. Cloudflare’s chief executive explained his decision to our columnist.

headline: Internet Traffic Dipped as Viewers Took in the Eclipse
snippet: Internet traffic dropped by 40 percent or more during the eclipse in states in the path of totality, including Maine, New Hampshire and Ohio, Cloudflare found.

headline: Cloudflare Introduces Default Blocking of A.I. Data Scrapers
snippet: The tech company’s customers can automatically block A.I. companies from exploiting their websites, it said, as it moves to protect original content online.

headline: Age-Gating the Internet + Cloudflare Takes On A.I. Scrapers + HatGPT
snippet: “Frankly, if they’re going to behave like hackers, then we’re going to behave like trolls right back to them,” said Matthew Prince, chief executive of Cloudflare.
byline: By Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Jen Poyant, Chris Wood, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Rowan Niemisto and Alyssa Moxley

headline: Cloudflare Says It Has Resolved Outage That Disrupted Parts of the Internet
snippet: Services from Cloudflare, a software company, underpin thousands of websites, including X, Spotify and OpenAI. The company said a crash in a software system was to blame.
